finding: During TASK-RFT-0052, the task scope and deliverables included scripts/validate-cli/**/*.ts, but node atm.mjs git commit --auto-stage committed only tracked delivery files and left newly created validate-cli shard files untracked. Later, taskflow close dry-run accepted two historical delivery refs, but close --write rejected the second ref because the same glob-matched shard files were classified as out-of-scope source files.
expectedBehavior: The governed commit bundle and historical-delivery close gate should share the same glob expansion semantics for allowed untracked files. Newly created in-scope shard files should be included and accepted, or the dry-run should fail closed with the same blocker that --write will enforce.
evidenceOrRepro: TASK-RFT-0052: commit 957917cee315295f6e0232a01bd6aea2fa032d26 omitted scripts/validate-cli/*.ts even though .atm/history/tasks/TASK-RFT-0052.json contained scripts/validate-cli/**/*.ts. Follow-up governed commit 57d10cc3e1140f16224c48f775acc2d44e898462 was required after manual explicit staging. taskflow close dry-run with both refs reported ready, while taskflow close --write rejected 57d10cc as no-scoped-deliverable-files and bucketed scripts/validate-cli/*.ts as outOfScopeSourceFiles.
